info.title: PgBeam Control Plane API
info.description:
REST API for the PgBeam multi-tenant PostgreSQL proxy platform.
Manages projects, database connections, cache/pool configuration, and regions.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Project lifecycle, custom domains, read replicas, and cache rules

tags.name: Policies
tags.description: Policy profiles enforced on agent credentials

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Scoped agent credentials for AI agent database access

tags.name: Approvals
tags.description: Human approval workflow for held agent statements

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Event delivery endpoints for project audit and anomaly events

tags.name: Anomalies
tags.description: Anomalous-behavior alerts surfaced by the gateway

tags.name: Branches
tags.description: Ephemeral sandbox branches for write isolation

tags.name: Migrations
tags.description: Migration safety linting for schema changes

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Usage metrics, billing data, and query insights

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account data, privacy controls, and onboarding

tags.name: MCP
tags.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) remote server endpoint

get.description: Returns all personal data associated with the authenticated user in a structured JSON format. Supports GDPR data portability and CCPA right to know. Audit logs are limited to the most recent 1000 entries.

post.description: Issues a scoped Postgres login and hosted MCP token for an AI agent. The connection string and MCP token are returned once and cannot be retrieved again.

201.description: Agent credential created. Secrets shown once.

403.description: Agent credential quota reached for the organization plan.

get.description: Lists agent credentials for the project. Secrets are never returned.
Returns a single agent credential. Secrets are never included.

patch.summary: Enable or disable an agent credential
patch.description: Toggles the kill-switch. Disabling drops live connections within seconds.

delete.description: Permanently revokes the credential and drops any live connections.

post.description: Generates a new Postgres password and MCP token for the credential in place, keeping the same id, username, name, and policy. Live connections using the old password are dropped within seconds. The new secrets are returned once and cannot be retrieved again.

200.description: Secrets rotated. New secrets shown once.

409.description: The credential is revoked and cannot be rotated.

get.description: Lists anomaly alerts for the project, newest first, optionally filtered by status.

patch.description: Updates the triage status of an anomaly alert (acknowledge or resolve).

get.description: Lists approval requests for the project, newest first, optionally filtered by status.

post.description: Approves a pending approval request, releasing the held statement.
Rejects a pending approval request, denying the held statement.

get.description: Returns agent statement audit entries for the project, newest first, with optional credential, event, decision, source and date-range filters.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Return entries strictly older than this timestamp (keyset pagination cursor).

get.summary: Export agent audit logs as CSV
get.description: Streams the project's agent audit entries as a CSV file, newest first, honoring the same credential, event, decision, source and date-range filters as the list endpoint. The full filtered set is streamed (no pagination); the result is suitable for spreadsheets, SIEM ingestion, and compliance archives.

200.description: CSV stream of filtered audit entries.

get.description: Recomputes the project's audit hash chain over an optional time range and reports whether it is intact. Each audit entry is linked to its predecessor with a SHA-256 hash, so editing or deleting any row breaks the chain. On a break, the response reports the first sequence number where a tampered or deleted entry was detected. Requires the same audit:read permission as the list and export endpoints.
Returns all available plan tiers with their limits and pricing.
Returns the current plan and limits for the organization.

Returns the Vercel Marketplace installation for the organization,
including its provisioned resources and their current-period usage.
Returns 404 when the organization was not provisioned through the
Vercel Marketplace. Powers the dashboard's Vercel integration page.

put.description: Sets the monthly spend limit for an organization. Null removes the limit.

post.description: Records optional feedback when a user cancels their subscription.

get.description: Lists ephemeral sandbox branches for the project's databases.

delete.description: Marks an ephemeral sandbox branch as discarded for teardown.

get.description:
Returns the cache rules for a database, showing all observed query
shapes with their stats and cache recommendations.

parameters.name: query_hash
parameters.description: xxhash64 hex of the normalized SQL.

put.description:
Enable or disable caching for a specific query shape, with optional
TTL and SWR overrides. Requires the query to exist in the cache rules.

post.description: Registers a new upstream PostgreSQL database for the project.

get.description: Lists all databases registered for the project.
Returns a single database by ID.

patch.description: Partially updates a database connection. Only provided fields are modified.

delete.description: Removes a database registration from the project.

post.description: Attempts to connect to the upstream database using the stored credentials and returns the result.

post.summary: Scan a database for likely-PII columns
post.description: Connects to the upstream database read-only, inspects information_schema and samples column values against PII heuristics, and returns ranked masking suggestions. Suggestions are advisory — the operator reviews them and applies the ones they want into a policy profile's masking rules. Nothing is auto-applied.

get.description: Connects to the upstream database read-only and returns its user relations (tables and views) and columns. Powers table/column autocomplete and view-aware warnings in the policy editor — relation kind distinguishes a view (whose masking/row-filters apply to the view itself, not its base tables) from a base table, and a per-column is_binary flag flags columns that mask to NULL. System schemas are excluded; nothing is persisted.

post.description: Registers a new custom domain for the project. Returns DNS verification instructions. Requires a Scale or Enterprise plan.

403.description: Custom domains require a Scale or Enterprise plan.

get.description: Lists all custom domains registered for the project.

delete.description: Removes a custom domain from the project and revokes its TLS certificate.

post.description: Checks DNS TXT record to verify domain ownership. Updates domain status on success. Requires a Scale or Enterprise plan.

403.description: Custom domains require a Scale or Enterprise plan.

get.description: Returns the health status of the API server.

get.summary: Get query insights for a project
get.description:
Returns aggregated query-level analytics for a project including top queries
by count, cache hit/miss summary, and latency statistics.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Time range to query. Defaults to 24h.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of top queries to return (1-100).

post.description:
Model Context Protocol endpoint using the Streamable HTTP transport. Accepts JSON-RPC 2.0 requests and exposes every public API operation as an MCP tool. Use this with any MCP-compatible client (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, custom agents) to let AI assistants manage PgBeam resources.

**Transport:** Streamable HTTP (MCP spec 2025-03-26)
**Protocol:** JSON-RPC 2.0
**Methods:** `initialize`, `tools/list`, `tools/call`, `ping`

Prefer the CLI (`pgbeam mcp`) for local stdio transport. Use this endpoint for remote or server-to-server MCP integration.
Analyzes a migration script for unsafe schema changes and returns findings.

get.description: Returns the onboarding checklist progress for an organization.

patch.description: Mark an onboarding step as complete or dismiss the checklist.

post.description: Creates a policy profile that can be attached to agent credentials.

get.description: Lists all policy profiles for the project.
Returns a single policy profile by ID.

put.description: Updates a policy profile. Changes hot-reload to active agent sessions.

delete.description: Deletes a policy profile. Fails if agent credentials still reference it.

409.description: Policy profile is still referenced by agent credentials.

post.summary: Dry-eval a policy against a SQL statement
post.description: Evaluates a single SQL statement against a policy (either a draft policy supplied inline or an existing policy referenced by id) and returns the decision the proxy would make: allow, block, mask, or row-filter. The evaluation reuses the data plane's own policy engine (the same parser, allow/block rules, row-filter rewriter, and masking analysis enforced on live agent sessions), so a what-if verdict matches real enforcement. Stateful checks a single-statement preview cannot model (per-region query and egress budgets, human approvals, and rollback/sandbox write routing) are reported as informational notes, not verdicts. This is a pure compute endpoint; it does not connect to the upstream database and persists nothing.

post.summary: Replay recorded agent traffic against a policy
post.description: Replays the project's recorded agent audit traffic against a candidate policy (either a draft supplied inline or an existing policy referenced by id) and reports what would change: which queries that ran would now be blocked, which blocked queries would now be permitted, and which results would be masked or row-filtered. Traffic is deduplicated by normalized query shape, newest first, and every statement is evaluated through the data plane's own policy engine, so the verdicts match real enforcement. Stateful checks (budgets, approvals, write-mode routing) are reported as informational notes on each result, not verdicts. This endpoint reads only the audit log; it never connects to the upstream database and persists nothing.

200.description: The replay summary and per-query decisions.

post.description: Creates a new project within the specified organization.

get.description: Lists projects filtered by organization. Requires org_id query parameter.
Returns a single project by ID.

patch.description: Partially updates a project. Only provided fields are modified.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a project and all associated databases.

get.description: Returns recent project metrics snapshots, optionally filtered by region.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent snapshots to return.

get.description: Returns all active data plane regions.
Lists all read replicas for a database.

post.description: Adds a read replica to a database.

delete.description: Removes a read replica from a database.

post.description: Issues an enrollment token a self-hosted (BYOC) proxy uses to authenticate to the control plane's config/audit gRPC stream. The token is returned once and cannot be retrieved again. Requires the Scale or enterprise plan.

403.description: The organization's plan is not entitled to self-host.

get.description: Lists an organization's self-host enrollments. Tokens are never returned.

delete.description: Revokes an enrollment so its token can no longer authenticate a proxy. Connected proxies keep their last-known config until they reconnect.

post.description: Mints a new enrollment token in place, keeping the same enrollment id, metadata, and expiry. The swap is atomic: the old token stops authenticating new proxy connections the moment this call returns. An already-connected proxy keeps its existing gRPC streams until it disconnects, then must present the new token to reconnect. The new token is returned once and cannot be retrieved again.

200.description: Token rotated. New token shown once.

409.description: The enrollment is revoked and cannot be rotated.

get.description: Lists support cases for the organization with optional status and search filters.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results per page (1-100, default 20).

post.description: Creates a new support case with an initial message.

get.description: Returns a support case with all its messages.

patch.description: Updates the status of a support case (close or reopen).

post.summary: Add a message to a support case
post.description: Adds a new message to an existing support case thread.
Receives a forwarded Slack event from the dashboard webhook handler and creates a support message.

get.description: Returns daily usage data aggregated across all projects in the organization.
Returns daily usage data for a specific project, broken down by region.

post.description: Creates a webhook endpoint that receives project event deliveries.

get.description: Lists webhook endpoints for the project.
Returns a single webhook endpoint by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a webhook endpoint and its pending deliveries.

post.description: Enqueues a test event delivery to the webhook endpoint.

DomainId.name: domain_id
DomainId.description: Unique custom domain identifier (prefixed, e.g. dom_xxx).

SupportCaseId.name: case_id
SupportCaseId.description: Unique support case identifier (prefixed, e.g. sc_xxx).

PolicyId.name: policy_id
PolicyId.description: Unique policy profile identifier (prefixed, e.g. pol_xxx).

AgentId.name: agent_id
AgentId.description: Unique agent credential identifier (prefixed, e.g. agt_xxx).

ApprovalId.name: approval_id
ApprovalId.description: Unique approval request identifier (prefixed, e.g. apr_xxx).

WebhookId.name: webhook_id
WebhookId.description: Unique webhook endpoint identifier (prefixed, e.g. whk_xxx).

AnomalyId.name: anomaly_id
AnomalyId.description: Unique anomaly alert identifier (prefixed, e.g. ano_xxx).

BranchId.name: branch_id
BranchId.description: Unique sandbox branch identifier (prefixed, e.g. brn_xxx).

PageSize.name: page_size
PageSize.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-100, default 20).

AuditCredentialId.name: credential_id
AuditCredentialId.description: Filter to a single agent credential.

AuditEvent.name: event
AuditEvent.description: Filter to a single event type (e.g. blocked, masked, query).

AuditDecision.name: decision
AuditDecision.description: Coarse outcome filter that groups events. `allow` = query; `block` = blocked, budget_exhausted, auth_failed, credential_expired; `mask` = masked; `truncate` = truncated.

AuditSource.name: source
AuditSource.description: Filter by statement origin (wire, mcp, rest, or control).

AuditStart.name: start
AuditStart.description: Return entries at or after this timestamp (inclusive lower bound).

AuditEnd.name: end
AuditEnd.description: Return entries strictly older than this timestamp (cursor / upper bound).

Forbidden.description: Operation not allowed by current plan limits.

Conflict.description: Resource already exists or conflicts with current state.

BearerAuth.description: JWT issued by Better Auth. Verified via JWKS.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key issued via the dashboard. Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>`.

InternalSecret.name: X-Internal-Secret
InternalSecret.description: Shared secret for dashboard-to-API service calls.

AccountExport.description: Full account data export for privacy and data-portability requests.

email_verified.description: Whether the email address has been verified.

created_at.description: When the user account was created.

updated_at.description: When the user profile was last updated.

slug.description: Organization slug used in URLs and routing.

created_at.description: When the organization membership was created.

projects.description: Projects owned through the user's organizations.

databases.description: Databases registered under the exported projects.

ssl_mode.description: PostgreSQL SSL mode in use for the database.

created_at.description: When the database registration was created.

sessions.description: Auth sessions associated with the user.

ip_address.description: IP address used to create the session, if known.

user_agent.description: User agent captured for the session, if known.

usage_limit_alerts.description: Whether usage alert emails are enabled.

consents.description: Recorded legal-document acceptances for the user.

document_type.description: Legal document type accepted by the user.

ip_address.description: IP address captured when consent was recorded.

audit_logs.description: Recent audit log entries associated with the account.

action.description: Action captured by the audit log entry.

resource_type.description: Type of resource affected by the action.

ip_address.description: IP address associated with the action, if known.

created_at.description: When the audit log entry was recorded.

AgentCredential.description: A PgBeam-issued, scoped Postgres login plus hosted MCP token for an AI agent.

policy_profile_id.description: Policy profile enforced for this credential.

pg_username.description: The Postgres username this credential presents to the proxy.

principal_type.description: Whether this credential represents an autonomous agent or a human operator.

auth_method.description: Postgres auth method the proxy presents for this credential.

last_used_at.description: When the credential was last used to connect, if ever.

expires_at.description: When the credential expires and becomes unusable. Null means it never expires. Enforcement is fail-closed in the proxy the instant this time passes, before any cleanup sweep runs.

updated_at.description: When the credential was last updated.

CreateAgentCredentialRequest.description: Request body for issuing a new agent credential.

policy_profile_id.description: The policy profile to enforce for this credential.

principal_type.description: Whether this credential represents an autonomous agent or a human operator.

expires_at.description: Optional expiry. When set, the credential becomes unusable at this time (must be in the future). Omit or set null for a credential that never expires.

AgentCredentialSecrets.description: One-time secrets returned only at creation. The connection string and MCP token are not retrievable again.

connection_string.description: Guarded Postgres connection string for this agent credential.

mcp_url.description: Hosted agent-database MCP endpoint for this project, served by the edge proxy on the same per-project host the agent connects to over the wire. Paste into an MCP client (Claude Code, Cursor, …) with the mcp_token as a bearer token.

mcp_token.description: Bearer token for the hosted MCP endpoint (shown once).

UpdateAgentCredentialStatusRequest.description: Request body for enabling or disabling an agent credential.

status.description: Set active to re-enable or disabled to kill-switch. Use DELETE to revoke permanently.

ListAgentCredentialsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of agent credentials for a project.

agents.description: Agent credentials on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

AnomalyAlert.description: A surfaced anomalous-behavior alert for review.

window_start.description: Start of the observation window, if applicable.

window_end.description: End of the observation window, if applicable.

acknowledged_at.description: When the alert was acknowledged, if any.

acknowledged_by.description: User who acknowledged the alert, if any.

UpdateAnomalyAlertRequest.description: Request body for triaging an anomaly alert.

status.description: New triage state for the alert.

ListAnomalyAlertsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of anomaly alerts for a project.

anomalies.description: Anomaly alerts on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

ApprovalRequest.description: A statement held by the gateway awaiting a human approval decision.

credential_id.description: Agent credential that submitted the statement, if known.

region.description: Data plane region that held the statement.

normalized_sql.description: Statement with literals replaced by placeholders.

statement_kind.description: Parsed statement kind (e.g. update, delete, ddl).

estimated_rows.description: Best-effort estimate of how many rows the statement would affect, attached by the data plane when the statement was held. Either the planner estimate from EXPLAIN or the exact count observed in a rolled-back trial execution. Null when no estimate could be made; treat it as an estimate, not a guarantee.

target_tables.description: Relations the statement touches, from the gateway's parse analysis.

status.description: Current state of the approval request.

reason.description: Human-readable note attached to a decision.

requested_at.description: When the statement was first held.

decided_at.description: When a decision was recorded, if any.

expires_at.description: When the request expires if undecided.

ApprovalDecisionRequest.description: Optional note attached to an approve/reject decision.

ListApprovalRequestsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of approval requests for a project.

approvals.description: Approval requests on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

AuditDecision.description: Coarse outcome filter that groups audit events. `allow` = query; `block` = blocked, budget_exhausted, auth_failed, credential_expired; `mask` = masked; `truncate` = truncated.

AuditSource.description: Statement origin (wire, mcp, rest, or control).

AuditLogEntry.description: One agent statement recorded by the gateway.

credential_id.description: Agent credential that issued the statement, if known.

region.description: Data plane region that served the statement.

event.description: Event type (query, blocked, masked, budget_exhausted, truncated, auth_failed, credential_expired).

normalized_sql.description: Statement with literals replaced by placeholders.

decision_rule.description: Machine-readable rule that produced a block, if any.

client_ip.description: Source IP of the agent connection.

source.description: Statement origin (wire, mcp, rest, or control for control-plane lifecycle events).

seq.description: Monotonic per-project position in the tamper-evident hash chain.

prev_hash.description: Hex SHA-256 of the previous chained entry (a fixed genesis value for the first entry). Empty for pre-chain legacy entries.

entry_hash.description: Hex SHA-256 of this entry's canonical content chained onto prev_hash. Empty for pre-chain legacy entries.

ListAuditLogsResponse.description: Time-ordered page of audit entries (newest first).

entries.description: Audit entries on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Cursor for the next page. Empty if no more results.

AuditChainVerification.description: Result of recomputing the project's tamper-evident audit hash chain over a time range. The chain links each entry to its predecessor, so editing or deleting any row breaks it.

ok.description: True when every chained entry in the range links correctly and no tampering or deletion was detected.

unchained_count.description: Pre-chain (legacy) entries in the range that predate the hash chain and were skipped rather than treated as tampering.

chain_start_seq.description: Sequence number of the first chained entry checked. Null when none.

chain_end_seq.description: Sequence number of the last chained entry checked. Null when none.

first_broken_seq.description: Sequence number where verification first failed (a tampered or deleted entry). Null when ok.

failure_reason.description: Machine-readable failure kind: hash_mismatch (entry content edited), broken_link (prev_hash does not match the predecessor), or missing_entry (a sequence number was deleted). Null when ok.

OrganizationPlan.description: Billing state and plan limits for an organization.

org_id.description: Organization identifier the plan applies to.

billing_provider.description: Which billing provider manages this organization's subscription.

enabled.description: Whether the organization billing is active (false = suspended).

custom_pricing.description: Whether the organization has custom enterprise pricing.

spend_limit.description: Monthly spend limit in dollars. Null means no limit.

spend_capped.description: Whether the organization has hit its spend limit and agent access is paused at the proxy. Lifts automatically when usage resets for the new billing period, or immediately when the spend limit is raised or removed.

spend_capped_at.description: When the spend cap was applied. Null when not capped.

created_at.description: When the billing record was created.

updated_at.description: When the billing record was last updated.

PlanLimits.description: Effective usage limits enforced for an organization plan.

queries_per_day.description: Maximum queries per day across all projects. 0 means unlimited.

queries_per_second.description: Maximum queries per second per project.

bytes_per_month.description: Monthly data transfer quota in bytes. 0 means unlimited.

max_query_shapes.description: Maximum query shapes in cache rules. 0 means unlimited.

included_seats.description: Number of seats included in the plan.

max_agent_credentials.description: Maximum agent credentials per organization. 0 means unlimited.

audit_retention_days.description: Agent audit-log retention window in days.

sandbox_max_branches.description: Maximum concurrently active instant (sandbox) branches. 0 means no limit.

sandbox_max_upstream_bytes.description: Maximum upstream source-database size a sandbox base sync accepts, in bytes. 0 means no limit.

sandbox_idle_seconds.description: Idle time before an instant (sandbox) branch scales to zero, in seconds.

sandbox_ttl_seconds.description: Lifetime of an instant (sandbox) branch before the TTL sweep discards it, in seconds.

PlanInfo.description: Publicly visible plan metadata and limits.

trial_days.description: Number of trial days for new signups.

ListPlansResponse.description: Response envelope for listing available billing plans.

plans.description: Billing plans available for self-serve signups.

UpdateSpendLimitRequest.description: Request body for updating an organization's monthly spend cap.

spend_limit.description: Monthly spend limit in dollars. Null to remove the limit.

CancellationFeedbackRequest.description: Request body for capturing cancellation feedback from a user.

VercelResourceStatus.description: A PgBeam project provisioned as a Vercel Marketplace resource, with current-period usage.

project_id.description: PgBeam project identifier backing the resource.

billing_plan_id.description: Plan tier the resource is billed on.

period_queries.description: Queries this resource has served in the current billing period.

period_bytes.description: Bytes transferred by this resource in the current billing period.

VercelInstallationStatus.description: Vercel Marketplace installation status for an organization, including its resources.

vercel_account_id.description: Vercel account/team identifier that owns the installation.

plan.description: Current plan tier for the organization.

DatabaseBranch.description: An ephemeral sandbox branch of a database used for write isolation.

database_id.description: Database this branch was created from.

credential_id.description: Agent credential that requested the branch, if known.

expires_at.description: When the branch expires, if set.

discarded_at.description: When the branch was discarded, if any.

ListDatabaseBranchesResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of sandbox branches for a project.

branches.description: Sandbox branches on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

CacheRuleEntry.description: Observed query shape and its cache recommendation for a database.

query_hash.description: xxhash64 hex of the normalized SQL.

normalized_sql.description: SQL with literals replaced by $N placeholders.

cache_enabled.description: Whether caching is enabled for this query shape.

cache_ttl_seconds.description: TTL override in seconds. Null means use project default.

cache_swr_seconds.description: SWR override in seconds. Null means use project default.

stability_rate.description: Response stability rate (0.0 to 1.0). Higher means more stable.

recommendation.description: Cache recommendation based on frequency, latency, and stability.

first_seen_at.description: When this query was first observed.

last_seen_at.description: When this query was last observed.

ListCacheRulesResponse.description: Cursor-paginated cache-rule entries for a database.

entries.description: Query shapes returned for the current page.

UpdateCacheRuleRequest.description: Request body for updating cache behavior of a query shape.

cache_enabled.description: Whether to enable caching for this query shape.

cache_ttl_seconds.description: TTL override in seconds. Null to use project default.

cache_swr_seconds.description: SWR override in seconds. Null to use project default.

UpdateCacheRuleResponse.description: Response returned after updating a cache rule.

Error.description: Standard error response envelope for PgBeam API requests.

error.description: Error metadata for the failed request.

Database.description: Registered upstream PostgreSQL database for a project.

connection_string.description: Connection string for connecting through PgBeam proxy. Password is replaced with a placeholder.

pool_region.description: Region where the connection pool is maintained (near the database). When set and different from the client's edge region, queries are relayed through the pool region's data plane. Empty means direct connection.

query_timeout_ms.description: Query timeout in milliseconds. When set (> 0), the proxy sends SET statement_timeout on upstream connections. 0 means disabled (default).

auto_read_routing.description: When enabled and replicas are configured, SELECT queries automatically route to read replicas without requiring the @pgbeam:replica SQL annotation.

created_at.description: When the database registration was created.

updated_at.description: When the database registration was last updated.

DatabaseRole.description: Database role. Primary receives writes, replicas receive reads.

enabled.description: Whether query caching is enabled. When false, all queries bypass the cache.

ttl_seconds.description: Cache TTL in seconds for query results.

max_entries.description: Maximum number of cached query results.

max_active.description: Maximum concurrent upstream connections per pool.

CreateDatabaseRequest.description: Request body for registering an upstream database.

password.description: PostgreSQL password. Stored encrypted at rest.

pool_region.description: Region where the connection pool is maintained (near the database). When set and different from the client's edge region, queries are relayed through the pool region's data plane. Empty means direct connection.

query_timeout_ms.description: Query timeout in milliseconds. 0 means disabled (default).

auto_read_routing.description: Auto-route SELECT queries to read replicas.

UpdateDatabaseRequest.description: Request body for partially updating an upstream database.

pool_region.description: Region where the connection pool is maintained (near the database). When set and different from the client's edge region, queries are relayed through the pool region's data plane. Empty means direct connection.

query_timeout_ms.description: Query timeout in milliseconds. 0 means disabled.

auto_read_routing.description: Auto-route SELECT queries to read replicas.

ListDatabasesResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of databases attached to a project.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

TestConnectionResult.description: Result of testing connectivity to an upstream database.

error.description: Error message if the connection test failed.

server_version.description: PostgreSQL server version string, if available.

CustomDomain.description: Custom database hostname attached to a project.

verified.description: Whether DNS ownership has been verified.

dns_verification_token.description: Token value for the DNS TXT record verification.

created_at.description: When the custom domain was created.

updated_at.description: When the custom domain was last updated.

CreateCustomDomainRequest.description: Request body for attaching a custom domain to a project.

domain.description: The custom domain name to add (e.g., db.example.com).

ListCustomDomainsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of custom domains for a project.

domains.description: Custom domains on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

DnsInstructions.description: DNS records the user needs to create for domain verification.

txt_host.description: The TXT record host for verification.

txt_value.description: The TXT record value for verification.

acme_cname_host.description: The CNAME record host for ACME TLS certificate issuance.

acme_cname_target.description: The CNAME target for ACME challenge delegation.

VerifyCustomDomainResponse.description: Result of checking DNS ownership for a custom domain.

HealthResponse.description: Health status and build metadata for the API service.

image_sha.description: Resolved image SHA (digest or SHA tag).

QueryInsight.description: Aggregated analytics for a normalized SQL query shape.

query_hash.description: xxhash64 of the normalized SQL pattern (hex).

total_count.description: Total number of executions in the time range.

total_cache_hits.description: Total cache hits attributed to the query shape.

total_cache_misses.description: Total cache misses attributed to the query shape.

CacheSummary.description: Aggregate cache performance metrics for the selected time range.

total_hits.description: Total number of cache hits in the selected time range.

total_misses.description: Total number of cache misses in the selected time range.

hit_rate.description: Cache hit rate as a fraction (0.0 to 1.0).

LatencySummary.description: Aggregate latency metrics for the selected time range.

avg_ms.description: Average latency in milliseconds across all queries.

p99_ms.description: P99 latency in milliseconds across all queries.

ProjectInsights.description: Query insights, cache performance, and latency for a project.

queries.description: Top query shapes observed for the project.

McpRequest.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 request envelope for MCP over Streamable HTTP.

McpResponse.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 response envelope for MCP over Streamable HTTP.

id.description: Mirrors the request id. Null for parse errors.

MigrationLintRequest.description: A migration script to lint for unsafe schema changes.

sql.description: The migration SQL to analyze. May contain multiple statements.

database_id.description: Optional database to scope the lint to.

MigrationFinding.description: A single issue found while linting a migration.

severity.description: How serious the finding is. error indicates an unsafe change.

statement.description: The statement that triggered the finding.

MigrationLintResponse.description: Result of linting a migration script.

safe.description: True when no error-severity findings were detected.

OnboardingProgress.description: Progress state for the dashboard onboarding checklist.

organization_id.description: Organization ID this onboarding belongs to.

org_created.description: Whether the organization has been created.

project_created.description: Whether a project has been created.

database_added.description: Whether a database has been added.

connection_tested.description: Whether the database connection has been tested.

connection_string_copied.description: Whether the connection string has been copied.

first_query_run.description: Whether the first query has been run through PgBeam.

agent_credential_created.description: Whether an agent credential has been issued.

dismissed.description: Whether the user has dismissed the onboarding checklist.

completed.description: Whether all onboarding steps are complete.

completed_at.description: When onboarding was completed. Null if not yet completed.

UpdateOnboardingRequest.description: Request body for advancing or dismissing onboarding.

step.description: The onboarding step to mark as complete.

dismiss.description: Set to true to dismiss the onboarding checklist.

PiiSuggestion.description: A single likely-PII column detected by the scanner, with a recommended masking rule. Suggestions are advisory only — nothing is applied until the operator reviews and adds it to a policy profile.

data_type.description: PostgreSQL data type of the column.

pii_type.description: The kind of personally identifiable information detected.

mask_kind.description: Recommended masking kind for this column. hash is suggested for join/group keys (e.g. email), redact for free-text PII, null for highly sensitive secrets.

confidence.description: Confidence score in [0,1]. Combines column-name signal and sampled-value signal; higher is stronger evidence.

reasons.description: Human-readable explanations for why the column was flagged.

sample_match_count.description: Number of sampled non-null values that matched the PII pattern.

sample_size.description: Number of non-null values sampled from the column.

ScanPiiResult.description: Result of a PII detection scan over a database's schema.

suggestions.description: Ranked masking suggestions, highest confidence first. Empty when no likely-PII columns were found.

truncated.description: True when the scan stopped early at the column limit and some columns were not inspected.

error.description: Present when the scan failed to connect or read the schema.

MaskingRule.description: A column masking rule applied to agent query results.

kind.description: How to mask. redact replaces with a token, null returns NULL, hash returns a SHA-256 hex.

RowFilter.description: A per-relation row filter. The predicate is a raw SQL boolean expression ANDed into the WHERE clause for the matching relation.

predicate.description: Raw SQL boolean expression ANDed into WHERE for this relation.

StatementRules.description: Per-statement-kind allow/deny lists. Empty allow means all kinds permitted by the access mode.

allow.description: Allowed statement kinds (select, insert, update, delete, ddl, copy, set, show, explain, transaction).

deny.description: Denied statement kinds (takes precedence over allow).

PolicyProfile.description: A named bundle of rules enforced on agent credentials in the data plane.

name.description: Human-readable name for the policy profile.

access_mode.description: read_only blocks all data and schema mutations.

table_allowlist.description: If non-empty, only these relations are reachable. Schema-qualified or bare names.

table_denylist.description: Relations explicitly blocked (takes precedence over the allowlist).

masking_rules.description: Column masking rules applied to query results.

budget_queries_per_hour.description: Max queries per rolling hour window. 0 means unlimited.

budget_queries_per_day.description: Max queries per day window. 0 means unlimited.

max_rows.description: Max rows returned per query. 0 means unlimited.

statement_timeout_ms.description: Upstream statement timeout for agent sessions. 0 uses the project default.

row_filters.description: Per-relation row filters ANDed into agent reads.

write_mode.description: How writes are handled. normal commits, rollback auto-rolls back, sandbox routes to an ephemeral branch.

approval_mode.description: Which statement classes require human approval before execution.

approval_auto_max_rows.description: Statements touching at most this many rows are auto-approved. 0 means none.

approval_timeout_seconds.description: How long a held statement waits for a decision before expiring.

migration_safety.description: Migration safety mode. warn surfaces findings, block refuses unsafe DDL.

egress_bytes_per_day.description: Per-day egress budget in bytes. 0 means unlimited.

max_affected_rows.description: Hard cap on rows a single write (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) may affect. A write whose affected-row count would exceed this is executed inside a transaction, checked, and rolled back so nothing persists, then blocked. Enforced independently of human approval. 0 means unlimited.

created_at.description: When the policy profile was created.

updated_at.description: When the policy profile was last updated.

PolicyProfileInput.description: Mutable fields of a policy profile (used for create and update).

name.description: Human-readable name for the policy profile.

access_mode.description: read_only blocks all data and schema mutations.

table_allowlist.description: If non-empty, only these relations are reachable.

masking_rules.description: Column masking rules applied to query results.

budget_queries_per_hour.description: Max queries per rolling hour window. 0 means unlimited.

budget_queries_per_day.description: Max queries per day window. 0 means unlimited.

max_rows.description: Max rows returned per query. 0 means unlimited.

statement_timeout_ms.description: Upstream statement timeout for agent sessions. 0 uses the project default.

row_filters.description: Per-relation row filters ANDed into agent reads.

write_mode.description: How writes are handled. normal commits, rollback auto-rolls back, sandbox routes to an ephemeral branch.

approval_mode.description: Which statement classes require human approval before execution.

approval_auto_max_rows.description: Statements touching at most this many rows are auto-approved. 0 means none.

approval_timeout_seconds.description: How long a held statement waits for a decision before expiring.

migration_safety.description: Migration safety mode. warn surfaces findings, block refuses unsafe DDL.

egress_bytes_per_day.description: Per-day egress budget in bytes. 0 means unlimited.

max_affected_rows.description: Hard cap on rows a single write (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) may affect. A write whose affected-row count would exceed this is executed inside a transaction, checked, and rolled back so nothing persists, then blocked. Enforced independently of human approval. 0 means unlimited.

ListPolicyProfilesResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of policy profiles for a project.

policies.description: Policy profiles on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

DryEvalInput.description: A SQL statement to test plus the policy to test it against. Supply exactly one of policy_id (an existing saved policy) or policy (an unsaved draft, e.g. the in-progress editor form).

sql.description: The single SQL statement to evaluate.
sql.example: SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = 1

policy_id.description: ID of an existing saved policy profile to evaluate against.

PolicyReplayInput.description: A window of recorded agent traffic to replay plus the candidate policy to replay it against. Supply exactly one of policy_id (an existing saved policy) or policy (an unsaved draft, e.g. the in-progress editor form).

policy_id.description: ID of an existing saved policy profile to replay against.

credential_id.description: Restrict the replay to traffic recorded for one agent credential.

start_ts.description: Start of the traffic window (inclusive). Defaults to 7 days before end_ts.

end_ts.description: End of the traffic window (exclusive). Defaults to now.

limit.description: Maximum number of distinct queries to replay, newest first. Traffic is deduplicated by normalized query hash before evaluation.

PolicyReplaySummary.description: Aggregate outcome of a policy replay.

entries_scanned.description: Recorded audit entries covered by the replayed queries.

distinct_queries.description: Distinct normalized queries found in the window (capped at limit).

evaluated.description: Distinct queries actually evaluated against the candidate policy.

would_allow.description: Queries the candidate policy would allow unchanged.

would_block.description: Queries the candidate policy would block.

would_mask.description: Queries the candidate policy would mask result columns on.

would_row_filter.description: Queries the candidate policy would inject a row filter into.

newly_blocked.description: Queries that executed under the recorded policy but the candidate policy would block.

newly_allowed.description: Queries that were blocked when recorded but the candidate policy would permit.

skipped_unparseable.description: Distinct queries skipped because the stored statement was truncated at record time and can no longer be parsed faithfully.

truncated.description: True when the window contained more distinct queries than limit; only the newest were replayed.

PolicyReplayItem.description: One distinct recorded query and the decision the candidate policy would reach for it.

query_hash.description: Normalized query hash identifying this statement shape.

sql.description: The recorded statement (normalized, literals replaced with $N placeholders), from its most recent occurrence.

statement_kind.description: Statement kind (select, insert, update, delete, ddl, ...).

occurrences.description: How many recorded entries share this query shape in the window.

recorded_event.description: The most recent recorded outcome for this query (query, masked, truncated, or blocked).

recorded_rule.description: The decision rule recorded with a blocked outcome.

change.description: How the candidate decision compares to the recorded outcome. newly_blocked and newly_allowed are the changes worth reviewing before rollout; still_allowed and still_blocked are unchanged.

PolicyReplayResult.description: The outcome of replaying recorded agent traffic against a candidate policy: an aggregate summary plus per-query decisions, changes first.

items.description: Per-query replay decisions, ordered changes first (newly blocked, then newly allowed), then by occurrence count.

DryEvalMaskedColumn.description: One result column that would be masked, with the mask kind applied.

column.description: The output column name (post-alias) the client would see masked.

DryEvalResult.description: The decision the proxy would reach for the supplied statement under the supplied policy. verdict is the headline outcome; the remaining fields detail why.

verdict.description: allow — permitted unchanged; block — rejected; mask — permitted but listed result columns are masked; row-filter — permitted but a WHERE predicate is injected (and any listed columns are also masked).

rule.description: Machine-readable decision tag. For a block, the blocking rule (e.g. read_only, table_denied, table_not_allowed, statement_denied, row_filter_unsafe). Otherwise "ok".

hint.description: What the author could do instead (present on block verdicts).

masked_columns.description: Result columns that would be masked. Present on mask/row-filter verdicts.

row_filter_predicate.description: The row-filter predicate ANDed into the statement's WHERE clause.

rewritten_sql.description: The statement as it would be sent upstream after row-filter injection.

notes.description: Informational caveats about stateful enforcement a single-statement preview cannot model (per-region budgets, approvals, write-mode routing).

Project.description: Project configuration and current control-plane state.

org_id.description: Better Auth organization ID that owns this project.

tags.description: User-defined labels applied to the project.

cloud.description: Cloud provider where the project runs.

self_hosted.description: When true, this project's data plane runs in the customer's own VPC/cluster (BYOC): the control plane does not provision hosted infra, and a self-hosted proxy dials home over the config/audit gRPC stream. Requires the Scale or enterprise plan.

proxy_host.description: Proxy hostname for connecting through PgBeam (e.g., myproject.proxy.pgbeam.app).

queries_per_second.description: Maximum queries per second for this project. 0 means unlimited.

burst_size.description: Burst allowance above the steady-state rate. 0 uses queries_per_second as burst.

max_connections.description: Maximum concurrent proxy connections. 0 means unlimited.

allowed_cidrs.description: IP filtering rules as CIDR ranges with optional labels. When non-empty, only connections from matching IPs are accepted. Empty array means all IPs are allowed (default). Both IPv4 (e.g. 10.0.0.0/8) and IPv6 (e.g. 2001:db8::/32) are supported.

default_policy_profile_id.description: When set, passthrough/human connections are enforced against this policy profile.

agents_disabled.description: Project-level kill-switch. When true, ALL agent-credential connections to this project are blocked at the proxy and live agent sessions are dropped within seconds. Passthrough/human connections are unaffected.

database_count.description: Number of databases attached to this project.

active_connections.description: Current active connections (latest metrics snapshot). 0 when no metrics data.

updated_at.description: When the project was last updated.

DataResidency.description: Data-residency requirement for the project. "any" (default) lets queries be served from the nearest data-plane metro. "us" or "eu" require the serving metro to be in that jurisdiction; the proxy fails a connection closed when it is served from a metro outside the required jurisdiction, so regulated workloads never process outside their permitted region.

CreateProjectRequest.description: Request body for creating a project and its primary database.

tags.description: User-defined labels to attach to the project.

self_hosted.description: Mark this project as running on a self-hosted (BYOC) data plane in the customer's own VPC/cluster. Requires the Scale or enterprise plan.

database.description: Primary database. Created atomically with the project.

CreateProjectResponse.description: Response returned after creating a project and its primary database.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Request body for partially updating a project.

tags.description: Replacement set of user-defined project labels.

allowed_cidrs.description: IP filtering rules as CIDR ranges with optional labels. Empty array means allow all. Both IPv4 and IPv6 CIDR notation are supported.

default_policy_profile_id.description: When set, passthrough/human connections are enforced against this policy profile. Send an empty string to clear.

agents_disabled.description: Project-level kill-switch. Set true to block ALL agent-credential connections to this project (live agent sessions are dropped within seconds); set false to re-enable them. Passthrough/human connections are unaffected. Engaging the kill-switch emits a kill_switch webhook event.

ListProjectsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of projects for an organization.

total.description: Total number of projects (across all pages).

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

CidrEntry.description: An IP filtering rule with a CIDR range and optional human-readable label.

cidr.description: CIDR range in IPv4 (e.g. 203.0.113.0/24) or IPv6 (e.g. 2001:db8::/32) notation. A plain IP without prefix length defaults to /32 (IPv4) or /128 (IPv6).

label.description: Optional human-readable label for this CIDR entry (e.g. "Office", "VPC").

Region.description: Data plane region available for PgBeam traffic.

available.description: Whether new projects can currently be assigned to the region.

continent.description: Continent where the region is located.

country.description: Country where the region is located.

ListRegionsResponse.description: Response envelope for available region queries.

regions.description: Regions currently known to the control plane.

Replica.description: Read replica registered for a primary database.

updated_at.description: When the replica was last updated.

CreateReplicaRequest.description: Request body for adding a read replica to a database.

ListReplicasResponse.description: Response envelope for listing registered replicas.

replicas.description: Read replicas attached to the database.

SchemaCatalogColumn.description: One column of a relation in the schema catalog.

is_binary.description: True for binary-typed columns (e.g. bytea). Masking a binary column always returns NULL — a redact/hash token would corrupt the wire type — so the editor warns when a masking rule targets one.

SchemaCatalogRelation.description: One relation (base table or view) in the schema catalog.

kind.description: Relation kind. Allowlists, masking, and row-filters are enforced against the named relation itself, NOT through a view to its base tables — so a view over a row-filtered base table can leak. The editor warns when a policy entry targets a view.

SchemaCatalog.description: A read-only snapshot of a database's user relations (tables and views) and their columns, used to power table/column autocomplete and view-aware warnings in the policy editor. System schemas (pg_catalog, information_schema, pg_toast) are excluded.

relations.description: User relations, ordered by schema then name.

scanned_relations.description: Number of relations in the catalog.

scanned_columns.description: Number of columns in the catalog.

truncated.description: True when the catalog stopped early at the column limit.

error.description: Present when introspection failed to connect or read the schema.

SelfHostEnrollment.description: An enrollment that lets a self-hosted (BYOC) proxy authenticate to the control plane's config/audit gRPC stream on behalf of an organization. The token itself is never returned after creation, only its metadata.

region_label.description: Operator-supplied label for where the proxy runs (informational).

created_by.description: User ID that created the enrollment.

last_seen_at.description: Last time a proxy authenticated with this enrollment.

revoked_at.description: When the enrollment was revoked. Null means active.

expires_at.description: When the enrollment token expires and stops authenticating new proxy connections. Null means it never expires. Enforcement is fail-closed at the gRPC auth gate the instant this time passes.

SelfHostEnrollmentSecret.description: Response returned once when an enrollment is created or its token is rotated. The token is shown a single time and cannot be retrieved again; set it as GRPC_AUTH_TOKEN on the self-hosted proxy.

token.description: The plaintext enrollment token (pbh_...). Shown once.

CreateSelfHostEnrollmentRequest.description: Request body for issuing a self-host enrollment token.

region_label.description: Operator-supplied label for where the proxy runs.

expires_at.description: Optional expiry. When set, the enrollment token stops authenticating new proxy connections at this time (must be in the future). Omit or set null for a token that never expires.

ListSelfHostEnrollmentsResponse.description: List of self-host enrollments for an organization.

enrollments.description: The organization's self-host enrollments, newest first.

SupportCase.description: A support case filed by an organization member.

slack_channel_id.description: Slack channel where the case thread lives.

slack_thread_ts.description: Slack thread timestamp for the case.

updated_at.description: When the case was last updated.

closed_at.description: When the case was closed, if applicable.

message_count.description: Number of messages in the case.

SupportCaseStatus.description: Current status of the support case.

SupportMessage.description: A message within a support case thread.

author_name.description: Display name of the message author.

CreateSupportCaseRequest.description: Request body for creating a support case.

UpdateSupportCaseRequest.description: Request body for updating a support case status.

CreateSupportMessageRequest.description: Request body for adding a message to a support case.

cases.description: Support cases for the current page.

total.description: Total number of cases matching the filter.

GetSupportCaseResponse.description: Support case with its message thread.

messages.description: Messages in the support case thread.

SlackEventPayload.description: Slack event forwarded from the dashboard webhook handler.

channel_id.description: Slack channel ID where the message was posted.

thread_ts.description: Slack thread timestamp identifying the support case thread.

user_id.description: Slack user ID of the message author.

ProjectMetricsResponse.description: Response envelope for recent project metrics snapshots.

snapshots.description: Metrics snapshots ordered from newest to oldest.

MetricsSnapshot.description: Aggregated project metrics captured for a point in time.

project_id.description: Project that produced the metrics snapshot.

region.description: Region where the traffic was served.

queries_total.description: Total queries served during the snapshot interval.

cache_hits.description: Number of queries served from cache.

avg_latency_ms.description: Average end-to-end query latency in milliseconds.

p99_latency_ms.description: 99th percentile end-to-end query latency in milliseconds.

avg_connect_ms.description: Average upstream connection acquisition latency in milliseconds.

avg_query_ms.description: Average upstream query execution latency in milliseconds.

UsageResponse.description: Response envelope for organization usage queries.

usage.description: Daily usage entries aggregated across the organization.

ProjectUsageResponse.description: Response envelope for project usage queries.

usage.description: Daily usage entries grouped by region for the project.

DailyUsage.description: Daily usage totals aggregated across all projects in an organization.

queries_total.description: Total queries served on the day.

cache_hits.description: Total cache hits on the day.

cache_misses.description: Total cache misses on the day.

connection_seconds.description: Total proxy connection time consumed on the day.

ProjectDailyUsage.description: Daily usage totals for a single project and region.

region.description: Region where the traffic was served.

queries_total.description: Total queries served on the day.

cache_hits.description: Total cache hits on the day.

cache_misses.description: Total cache misses on the day.

connection_seconds.description: Total proxy connection time consumed on the day.

WebhookEndpoint.description: A delivery target for project audit/event notifications.

url.description: HTTPS endpoint that receives event deliveries.

event_types.description: Event types to deliver. Empty means all events.

items.description: An event type (e.g. blocked, anomaly, approval).

enabled.description: Whether deliveries are active for this endpoint.

updated_at.description: When the endpoint was last updated.

WebhookEndpointInput.description: Mutable fields of a webhook endpoint (used for create and update).

url.description: HTTPS endpoint that receives event deliveries.

secret.description: Shared secret used to sign delivery payloads. Write-only.

event_types.description: Event types to deliver. Empty means all events.

enabled.description: Whether deliveries are active for this endpoint.

WebhookTestResponse.description: Result of sending a synthetic test event to a webhook endpoint.

status.description: Outcome of the test delivery (delivered or failed).

status_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the endpoint, if a response was received.

error.description: Error detail when the test delivery failed.

ListWebhookEndpointsResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of webhook endpoints for a project.

webhooks.description: Webhook endpoints on the current page.

next_page_token.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

Project.description: Manages a PgBeam project with a primary database.

primary_database_id.description: ID of the primary database created with this project.

Database.description: Manages an upstream database connection within a PgBeam project.

Replica.description: Manages a read replica for a PgBeam database.

CustomDomain.description: Manages a custom domain for a PgBeam project.

CacheRule.description: Manages a per-query cache rule. Deletion disables caching (soft-delete).

SpendLimit.description: Manages the monthly spend limit for an organization.

AgentCredential.description: Manages a scoped agent credential (a PgBeam-issued Postgres login plus a hosted MCP token) for an AI agent. The connection string and MCP token are one-time secrets returned only at creation and exposed as sensitive computed outputs; they cannot be retrieved again. To rotate the secrets, taint/replace the resource (or use the rotate endpoint out of band).

connection_string.description: One-time guarded Postgres connection string for this agent credential (shown once).

mcp_url.description: Hosted agent-database MCP endpoint for this project. Use with the mcp_token as a bearer token.

mcp_token.description: One-time bearer token for the hosted MCP endpoint (shown once).

PolicyProfile.description: Manages a policy profile: a named bundle of agent-gateway enforcement rules (access mode, table allow/deny lists, statement-kind rules, PII masking rules, per-relation row filters, query/egress budgets, write mode, approvals, and migration safety) attached to agent credentials and enforced in the PG wire protocol. Nested-list fields (masking_rules, row_filters) and the nested statement_rules object are expressed as structured config.

WebhookEndpoint.description: Manages a webhook endpoint that receives project audit and anomaly event deliveries. The signing secret is write-only and never returned by the API.

SelfHostEnrollment.description: Manages a self-host (BYOC) enrollment: a token a self-hosted proxy uses to authenticate to the control plane's config/audit stream. The token is a one-time secret returned only at creation and exposed as a sensitive computed output; it cannot be retrieved again. To rotate the token, replace the resource. Deletion revokes the enrollment.

token.description: One-time enrollment token (pbh_...) for the self-hosted proxy's GRPC_AUTH_TOKEN (shown once).
